mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so monthly declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a month-over-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent short-term threshold signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, which should compress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal’s out-of-sample Rank IC is not negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS strengthen together, it indicates broad consumer demand and hiring momentum, which should lift near-term corporate revenue expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day returns, or if triggered joint-strength periods do not beat baseline returns at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial production and slower real activity, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through lower earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in INDPRO, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year effect flips positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates are deeply negative and financial conditions are loose, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy-rate condition does not precede positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not above unconditional baseline at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST signals housing-cycle weakness and future demand contraction, which should weigh on cyclical earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the effect is indistinguishable from zero after controlling for baseline trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a fall in UNRATE confirms genuine labor-market tightening, which should improve growth expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered periods fail to produce positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 confirms real activity is rebounding and should lift equity cash-flow expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 accelerates versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 acceleration fails to align with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year tests.
